job_description:
SQL Server Developer (Mid-Level) – SSIS & SSRS

Experience: 5–7 Years Location: Hyderabad/Bangalore Employment Type: Full-Time

Job Summary

We are seeking a skilled Mid-Level SQL Server Developer with strong expertise in T-SQL, SSIS, and SSRS to support the development and optimization of enterprise data solutions. The ideal candidate will be responsible for building efficient ETL pipelines, developing high-quality reports, and ensuring reliable data processing across environments. This role requires a strong foundation in database development, data warehousing concepts, and deployment practices.

Key Responsibilities

  1. Database Development
  2. Develop, optimize, and maintain T-SQL queries, stored procedures, and database objects
  3. Ensure high performance and responsiveness of database systems

  4. Troubleshoot and resolve data-related issues

  5. ETL Development (SSIS)

  6. Design, develop, and maintain SSIS packages
  7. Work with data flow tasks, control flow, variables, parameters, and configurations

  8. Build scalable and efficient ETL pipelines for data integration

  9. Reporting (SSRS)

  10. Design and develop SSRS reports including Tablix, charts, and dashboards
  11. Implement report parameters, expressions, subscriptions, and security

  12. Collaborate with stakeholders to understand reporting requirements

  13. Deployment & Scheduling

  14. Manage deployments across Development, Test, and Production environments
  15. Configure and maintain SQL Server Agent jobs for scheduling and automation

  16. Ensure smooth release and version control processes

  17. Data Warehousing

  18. Work with data warehousing concepts such as star and snowflake schemas
  19. Implement Slowly Changing Dimensions (SCDs), Change Data Capture (CDC), staging, and incremental loads
  20. Support data modeling and data integration efforts

Required Skills & Qualifications

  • 5–7 years of experience in Microsoft SQL Server development
  • Strong expertise in T-SQL (queries, stored procedures, performance tuning)
  • Hands-on experience with SSIS (ETL development)
  • Hands-on experience with SSRS (report development)
  • Experience with SQL Server Agent for job scheduling
  • Knowledge of deployment processes across multiple environments
  • Familiarity with Git or other version control systems

Preferred Skills

  • Experience with Azure Data Platform (Azure SQL, Azure Data Factory)
  • Exposure to Azure Synapse Analytics or SSIS Integration Runtime (SSIS-IR)
  • Familiarity with Databricks
  • Experience with Power BI for data visualization
  • Attention to detail and commitment to quality

Contact: Share your resume at premithas@idatalytics.com or contact +91 9946843935 for more details.
summary: Mid-Level SQL Server Developer with 5–7 years of experience in T-SQL, SSIS, and SSRS to build ETL pipelines, reports, and ensure reliable data processing.

job_description:
Organic Growth & SEO Specialist

2Base Technologies is building its marketing function from a clear brief, and this role owns the organic search engine that sits at the centre of it.

We are a technology services company with over 15 years of delivery experience, working with mid-market and enterprise clients across the United States and the United Kingdom. Our work spans AI implementation, custom software development, legacy system modernisation, and operational transformation.

What This Role Exists to Do

This role does not optimise content after it is written. It determines what gets written, why, and how it is structured so that the right people find it at the right moment in their decision process.

The organic search function at 2Base is being built to generate qualified inbound pipeline from two specific markets, the US and UK, at scale. That means a large volume of high-intent pages, a technical foundation that performs, and a programmatic architecture that compounds over time. The person who fills this role designs that system and owns its results.

This is a strategic and technical role. It is not an SEO support role, a blogging role with keyword research added on, or a task-execution position within a larger team. If that distinction matters to you, read on.

What You Will Own

  • The organic traffic strategy for the US and UK markets, including keyword targeting and the full architecture of how search drives inbound pipeline
  • Keyword research and search intent mapping across all service lines and target audiences
  • Page architecture of the website, including what pages exist, what they target, how they are structured, and how they connect through internal linking
  • On-page SEO across all published pages
  • Technical SEO oversight, working directly with the development team on crawlability, indexing, site speed, Core Web Vitals, and structured data
  • Google Search Console as a working instrument, including weekly review, issue resolution, and opportunity identification
  • The programmatic SEO build, including designing the framework and templates that allow the site to scale to 80 to 120+ high-intent pages
  • SEO briefs written clearly enough that the Content Specialist can execute without guessing
  • Link building target approval. The outreach team identifies candidates, and you approve or reject based on defined quality standards
  • SEO performance reporting, including organic traffic, keyword movement, pages published, and Search Console health

What You Will Not Own

  • Writing content: you brief the Content Specialist, you do not write the pages
  • Link building outreach: a dedicated team member executes this, you set the standard
  • Paid search: that is a separate role
  • Social media distribution: separate channel, separate owner
  • Content calendar scheduling: the Marketing & Growth Lead sets distribution priorities

Requirements

Experience

  • Minimum 3 to 5 years in an SEO or organic growth role, with strong candidates typically at the 4 to 6 year mark
  • Experience ranking high-intent commercial pages, not just informational content
  • Experience managing technical SEO independently
  • Experience building or contributing to a programmatic SEO architecture
  • Strong working knowledge of Google Search Console as an active tool
  • Experience working across US and/or UK markets
  • Working knowledge of AEO, GEO, and AIO, including structuring content for AI-powered search surfaces
  • Ability to write SEO briefs that a content writer can execute without clarification

Mindset

  • Thinks in systems, not tasks. The goal is a compounding organic engine, not a checklist
  • Comfortable with ambiguity in early-stage environments
  • Analytically honest, able to diagnose what is not working and adjust direction independently
  • Collaborative but not passive. Works closely with content and development teams while driving the agenda

What We Are Not Looking For

  • SEO practitioners whose entire experience is informational content with no connection to commercial intent or pipeline
  • Anyone who treats Search Console as a monthly reporting task
  • Candidates who cannot explain the difference between high-intent and informational keyword targeting
  • Candidates whose SEO process depends entirely on a tool telling them what to do next
  • Candidates with no exposure to US or UK search behaviour who treat all markets as equivalent

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: Strategic and technical SEO leader to design a scalable organic growth system for US/UK markets, driving high-intent traffic through technical execution and programmatic architecture.

job_description:
Business Analyst

The Business Analyst plays a key role in ensuring requirements are clearly understood, accurately documented, and traceable throughout the software development lifecycle. This role focuses on translating business and functional inputs into structured, standardized documentation that supports effective development, testing, and delivery. Working closely with business analysts, design teams, and technical stakeholders, the role ensures consistency, clarity, and alignment across all requirement artifacts.

Key Responsibilities

  • Create Software Requirement Specifications (SRS) based on inputs from Core Business Analysts, stakeholder discussions, and requirement walkthroughs
  • Prepare Business Requirement Documents (BRD) using approved templates while ensuring business intent and system behavior are clearly documented
  • Participate in requirement discussions to understand business objectives, functional flows, dependencies, and assumptions before documentation
  • Analyze requirements to identify gaps, ambiguities, and inconsistencies, and work with Core BAs to resolve them
  • Document functional flows, business rules, and acceptance criteria where applicable to support development and QA teams
  • Document wireframes, UI flows, validations, and UI specifications in collaboration with design and product teams
  • Maintain change logs, version history, and document updates throughout the project lifecycle
  • Create and manage traceability matrices linking business, functional, and technical requirements
  • Maintain cross-references across BRD, SRS, UI, API, and related documentation
  • Ensure documentation consistency, completeness, and alignment with approved scope and delivery timelines
  • Ensure accurate and timely PMS updates with structured documentation details
  • Support development, QA, UAT, and audit processes through clear, traceable, and up-to-date documentation
  • Assist in requirement reviews, walkthroughs, and validation sessions with internal and client stakeholders

Required Skills and Experience

  • 1 to 3 years of experience in Business Analysis or related roles
  • Strong experience in BRD and SRS creation
  • Hands-on experience with documentation templates, standards, and version control
  • Ability to document UI wireframes and functional flows clearly
  • Working knowledge of APIs and system integrations for documentation purposes
  • Experience with project management systems and documentation tools
  • Strong attention to detail and ability to manage multiple documents in parallel
  • Excellent written communication and stakeholder coordination skills

Minimum Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Engineering, Computer Science, Information Systems, or a related field
  • 1 to 3 years of experience as a Business Analyst or Product Analyst

Benefits

  • A growth-driven environment with opportunities to lead, innovate, and upskill continuously.

If this opportunity aligns with your career goals, kindly share your updated resume with us at careers@2basetechnologies.com.
summary: The Business Analyst translates business requirements into structured documentation, ensuring clarity and traceability across development. Requires 1-3 years of experience in BRD/SRS creation and strong documentation skills.
